commands: cd apps/desktop/src-tauri; cargo test sidecar_process_tree_kill_command --test sidecar_service
interpretation: Regression proof for the installer QA cleanup finding. Tauri-managed sidecar stop now attempts Windows process-tree termination with taskkill /PID <pid> /T /F before falling back to Child::kill/wait, so PyInstaller descendant processes are covered in the managed desktop path.
remaining_limits: This is a managed sidecar supervision fix, not code signing or release-channel proof.
The previous installer QA evidence still records that direct manual sidecar launch can leave a PyInstaller child if the launcher is stopped without process-tree cleanup.
